{"idea": {"Name": "geo_precision_router", "Title": "Spectral Condition Routing: Training-Free Geometric Diagnostics for Hardware-Aware Mixed-Precision Inference", "Short Hypothesis": "The local geometric stability of transformer activations—quantified by the covariance condition number, SVD decay rate, Fisher discriminant ratio, and Hessian spectral radius—directly dictates layer-wise quantization sensitivity. By mapping these training-free spectral diagnostics to a hardware-aware precision dispatch table, we can dynamically route computations through FP16, INT8, or INT4 kernels without calibration or retraining. This setting is optimal because it bypasses costly calibration passes and heuristic error metrics, directly linking intrinsic manifold geometry to provable PAC-Bayes risk bounds, hyperparameter stability radii, and zero-overhead tensor core dispatch.", "Related Work": "Existing mixed-precision and post-training quantization (PTQ) frameworks (e.
